compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (signed) on iteration 3 after 2 remediations: (1) reframed to metadata-only classifier respecting the multi-seam reality; (2) dropped the dangling typed-error-subclass deliverable + made every mapping row a concrete matchable artifact. Implemented + committed ed47d9289. Advisory triage (15 advisories, all addressed in CODE to preserve the signed attestation rather than re-editing the description): E6/F1 err.outcome -> added runner-wiring test; E1/T8 exc-required -> made exc default None (finish_reason-only classification); G6/G1G2 context-length -> local hint list documented as mirroring sizing.is_context_limit_error + preservation test; T3 ordering -> ModelHTTPError matched before ModelAPIError (comment+covered); E2/T8 hex/base64 thresholds -> impl uses {32,}/{40,}; T8 diagnostic keys -> _ALLOWED_FIELDS canonical set; T8 placeholder -> {exception_type,message:'diagnostic unavailable'}. JUSTIFIED-not-fixed: T1 lint.py redactor reuse — kept failure.py's redactor self-contained; the two serve different surfaces (workflow-YAML secret-scan-for-display vs LLM-diagnostic inline-scrub) and coupling the low-level classifier boundary (drake imports it) to workflow/lint.py would be a worse dependency. T1 httpx-not-declared — httpx is guaranteed present transitively (pydantic-ai-slim/anthropic) wherever this agents-gated module is reachable. 38 new tests green; arawana matrix + 71 runner/structured tests green; mypy clean. Not closing until Verified +1.

compiled_state.description:
Classify every LLM failure into the closed resolution-disposition enum paired with a sanitized diagnostic (verbatim within a field allowlist), delivered as a PURE, total library function — the vocabulary + mapping the later stories wire in, without changing any exception the runner raises today.

## Why
`src/rebar/llm/runner.py` flattens auth, 429, 529, timeout, and content-filter into one `LLMUnavailableError` — no caller can tell "retry" from "buy quota" from "fix config" from "the agent is looping." Gates, exit codes, messaging, and telemetry need a typed disposition plus the raw truth for troubleshooting. This story delivers that CLASSIFICATION as a side-effect-free function; the per-seam wiring, the exit-code bifurcation, and any NEW raised error types are story authorial-hated-blackbear's (2eca) — it is the named owner of runner.py reconciliation and the only story that changes control flow.

## The multi-seam reality (why this is metadata-only)
The RED baseline (story arawana, committed a63ae39c) empirically pins that failures do NOT all pass through one `except`:
- `UsageLimitExceeded` is intercepted at a DEDICATED prior arm (`runner.py:335`) and re-raised as `LLMRunnerError`, BEFORE the generic `except Exception` (`runner.py:353-365`).
- `content_filter`/`length` `finish_reason` never raise from the model layer — the structured path's `structured.check_stop_reason` raises `UnretryableOutputError` (`structured.py:243`).
- rebar's own typed errors pass through unchanged at `runner.py:351` (`except LLMError: raise`).
- `sizing.is_context_limit_error(exc)` (sizing.py:171) matches on the exception MESSAGE to drive the model-escalation ladder; `pass1_with_ladder` (sizing.py:229) branches on `LLMUnavailableError` vs other.
So this story changes NO raised type anywhere; it adds a classifier + a metadata object. A pure classifier CAN classify a `UsageLimitExceeded` even though the generic seam never receives one — because callers (the RED suite; story drake; blackbear's future wiring) invoke the function DIRECTLY with any signal.

## What — five constructs, all in `src/rebar/llm/failure.py`
- `class ResolutionClass(str, Enum)` — the closed 8-class vocabulary: WAIT_AND_RETRY, RETRY_NOW, INCREASE_PROVIDER_LIMITS, CHANGE_SETTINGS, CHANGE_INPUT, CHANGE_PROVIDER_OR_MODEL, FIX_AGENT_DESIGN, NEEDS_INVESTIGATION.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class LLMOutcome{resolution_class, diagnostic: dict, retryable: bool}` — only `retryable` (True iff class in {WAIT_AND_RETRY, RETRY_NOW}) is behaviour-critical; the class is the messaging/telemetry hint.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ClassifyContext{model, provider, attempts, trace_id, finish_reason}` — all optional; `classify_llm_failure(exc)` works with none. `finish_reason` lets the structured seam classify a content-filter/truncation signal that raised no exception.
- `def classify_llm_failure(exc, ctx=None) -> LLMOutcome` — the pure, TOTAL classifier (never raises; an unmatched failure or an internal/sanitizer error degrades to NEEDS_INVESTIGATION with a minimal diagnostic).
- `def sanitize_diagnostic(fields) -> dict` — the allowlist + redactor (below).

NO new exception classes are introduced in this story (that is blackbear's, alongside the raise-site + exit-code changes). `errors.py` is NOT edited here. Back-compat is therefore trivial and total: this story raises nothing new, so every existing `except LLMUnavailableError` and every exit-code consumer is unaffected — asserted by a test that classifies each RED signal and confirms `classify_llm_failure` is additive (it returns metadata; it does not raise).

### Signal → resolution_class mapping (the classifier's COMPLETE vocabulary — every row a CONCRETE, matchable artifact)
Callers invoke the function directly with any of these; the RED suite pins each row by direct call, independent of where the classifier is wired.
| Concrete signal (exception type / status+body / finish_reason) | resolution_class | retryable |
|---|---|---|
| `ModelHTTPError` status 429 (body code NOT `insufficient_quota`); 529; 500/502/503/504 | WAIT_AND_RETRY | yes |
| `httpx.ReadTimeout` / other `httpx.TimeoutException` (read/pool/write) | WAIT_AND_RETRY | yes |
| `httpx.ConnectError`; `httpx.ConnectTimeout`; `ModelAPIError` with no `status_code` | RETRY_NOW | yes |
| `ModelHTTPError` 429 body code `insufficient_quota`; status 402 | INCREASE_PROVIDER_LIMITS | no |
| `ModelHTTPError` 401/403; malformed 400 (no context-length hint); `UserError` | CHANGE_SETTINGS | no |
| `ModelHTTPError` 400 with context-length hint (`is_context_limit_error` phrasing); 413; `finish_reason=="content_filter"` (via ctx); `ContentFilterError` | CHANGE_INPUT | no |
| `FallbackExceptionGroup` (provider/model fallback chain exhausted) | CHANGE_PROVIDER_OR_MODEL | no |
| `UsageLimitExceeded` (diagnostic records request- vs tool-call-limit); `IncompleteToolCall` | FIX_AGENT_DESIGN | no |
| anything else | NEEDS_INVESTIGATION | no |

- Matching is by concrete type / `status_code` / body `error.type|code` / `finish_reason` ONLY — no vague "blip"/"exhausted" prose triggers. rebar's OWN `StructuredOutputError`/`UnretryableOutputError` (validation-exhaustion, truncation) are NOT this classifier's inputs — they are handled at the structured seam and pass through `runner.py:351` unchanged.
- **Unwrap `tenacity.RetryError` FIRST** (`RetryError.last_attempt.exception()`) before matching, so an exhausted-retry `httpx.ReadTimeout`/`ConnectTimeout` maps correctly (regression-tested). `import tenacity` is GUARDED (`try/except ImportError`) → a safe no-op in wave 1 (tenacity arrives with story arcticduck/95a5).
- Discriminate `UnexpectedModelBehavior` subclasses (`ContentFilterError`, `IncompleteToolCall`) rather than the base.

### The single metadata-only runner touch (this story)
At the generic `except Exception` seam (`runner.py:353-365`) ONLY, call `classify_llm_failure(exc)` and attach the `LLMOutcome` to the raised `LLMUnavailableError` as an attribute (e.g. `err.outcome`), WITHOUT changing that it raises `LLMUnavailableError`. The `UsageLimitExceeded` arm, the `except LLMError` passthrough, the structured `UnretryableOutputError` path, and `is_context_limit_error` are LEFT EXACTLY AS-IS. Wiring the classifier at the other seams + onto verdict/coverage + exit codes is blackbear's job.

### Diagnostic sanitization (concrete mechanism, not just an ADR)
The diagnostic is built from an ALLOWLIST (exception type, status_code, provider error `code`/`type`, finish_reason, Retry-After, attempts, trace_id, model, provider, request/tool-call-limit flags) — the raw provider body is NEVER dumped wholesale. Any captured free-text passes through a single pure redactor masking `Authorization`/`x-api-key`/`api-key`/`cookie` values, bearer tokens, key-shaped tokens (`sk-...`, `sk-ant-...`, long hex/base64), and emails. Unit-tested against a planted secret. The ADR is RECORDED by blackbear (2eca, sole ADR/docs owner).

## Scope
- The five constructs above, all in `src/rebar/llm/failure.py` (stable path; story drake imports from here — a layout change is a breaking inter-child contract coordinated via blackbear).
- The single metadata-only runner touch (attach `LLMOutcome` at the generic seam; change no raised type).
- Best-effort provider-body inspection for the terminal-vs-transient 429 split (behind the reliable `retryable` core; a misread wastes <= N bounded retries, never a safety issue).
- NOT in scope: any NEW exception classes / raise-site changes / `errors.py` edits (blackbear); the retry mechanism (arcticduck); exit-code mapping, verdict/coverage plumbing, gate posture (blackbear).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `classify_llm_failure` maps every row of the table above to the correct `resolution_class` + `retryable`, pinned by a test that calls it DIRECTLY with each concrete signal (including `UsageLimitExceeded` and `finish_reason=="content_filter"`); an unrecognized exception -> NEEDS_INVESTIGATION.
- [ ] Catches `httpx.*` directly (ConnectTimeout is NOT a ConnectError — asserted); discriminates `ContentFilterError`/`IncompleteToolCall`; unwraps `tenacity.RetryError` first (a test wraps an `httpx.ReadTimeout` in a `RetryError` and asserts WAIT_AND_RETRY), with the `import tenacity` guarded so wave-1 (no tenacity) still passes.
- [ ] This story raises NO new exception type; a test asserts `classify_llm_failure` is additive metadata and that the existing `except LLMUnavailableError` catches + exit-code consumers are unchanged (nothing new is raised, `is_context_limit_error` still True for context-length, `check_stop_reason` still raises `UnretryableOutputError`).
- [ ] The diagnostic is sanitized (a test injects a fake API key / auth header in a provider body and asserts it is scrubbed before any sink), and a `sanitize_diagnostic` failure does not make the classifier raise (degrades to a placeholder).
- [ ] The classifier is total and never raises (property test over arbitrary exceptions, incl. one whose `__str__` raises).
- [ ] The metadata-only runner touch attaches the `LLMOutcome` at the generic seam without altering the raised type; the arawana RED assertions and the sizing/structured tests stay green; `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/failure.py` (new — all five constructs), `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (attach `LLMOutcome` at the generic seam only), `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_classifier.py` (new — table + sanitizer + totality + preservation tests).

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, tenacity 9.1.4, httpx 0.28.1, offline)
Run offline (ACTUAL captured output; no billable call). Confirms the symbols the classifier
discriminates and the tenacity unwrap the design pins:
```
[1] pydantic_ai.exceptions MROs:
    ModelHTTPError           OK   ['ModelAPIError', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    ModelAPIError            OK   ['AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError', 'Exception']
    ContentFilterError       OK   ['UnexpectedModelBehavior', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    IncompleteToolCall       OK   ['UnexpectedModelBehavior', 'AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError']
    UsageLimitExceeded       OK   ['AgentRunError', 'RuntimeError', 'Exception']
    UserError                OK   ['RuntimeError', 'Exception', 'BaseException']
    FallbackExceptionGroup   OK   ['ExceptionGroup', 'BaseExceptionGroup', 'Exception']
    ContentFilterError <: UnexpectedModelBehavior: True
    IncompleteToolCall <: UnexpectedModelBehavior: True
    FallbackExceptionGroup MRO: ['ExceptionGroup', 'BaseExceptionGroup', 'Exception']
[2] ModelHTTPError.status_code=429 body-error-type='insufficient_quota'  (terminal-vs-transient 429 reads body)
[3] httpx: [ConnectError, ConnectTimeout, ReadTimeout]; ReadTimeout <: TimeoutException: True (caught directly)
[4] tenacity.RetryError.last_attempt.exception() -> httpx.ReadTimeout: read timed out  (the unwrap)
[5] tenacity reraise=True -> httpx.ConnectTimeout (NOT RetryError: True)  (normal transport path)
[6] UsageLimitExceeded <: UnexpectedModelBehavior: False | <: AgentRunError: True  (separate FIX_AGENT_DESIGN bucket)
```
These are pydantic-ai/httpx/tenacity LIBRARY symbols, not rebar symbols — a code-grounding pass
restricted to the rebar snapshot must not read their repo-absence as non-existence (bug
succinct-formable-kite). The RED baseline (story arawana, committed) already pins the CURRENT
collapse these cases flip: HTTP/timeout/refusal/incomplete -> LLMUnavailableError today.

file_impact.reason: new: ResolutionClass, LLMOutcome, ClassifyContext, classify_llm_failure, sanitize_diagnostic
attach LLMOutcome at the generic except seam (metadata only; no raised-type change)
classifier table + sanitizer + totality + preservation tests

compiled_state.title: Classify LLM failures into the resolution-disposition enum + verbatim diagnostic
